artifact: ARCANOS Phase 2E MCP consultation status and independent advisory review

consultation.reason: The configured ARCANOS stdio command first reads the real Railway OPENAI_API_KEY and starts an OpenAI-initialized MCP server. No documented architecture-review tool is both advisory and read-only. Launching or calling an unknown tool would violate the safe-use boundary.

gateAIndependentReviewResult.scope: static design review only; implementation, migration, Railway, push, and production remain separately gated

independentReview.finding: Durable server-selected ownership must exist before any action side effect. A unique constraint without a conditional state transition is insufficient.
recommendations: Create one authoritative run per action before execution.
Claim through a database compare-and-swap transition.
Perform zero execution before the owner and realm are durable.
independentReview.reviewKind: sub-agent advisory; not ARCANOS backend-AI output

independentReview.finding: Result authentication and replay must resolve all authority from the stored run plus authenticated context.
recommendations: Do not trust owner, executor kind, realm, plan, lifecycle, or CLEAR fields from a result body.
Finalize an allowed active run state through compare-and-swap.
Return exact replay and conflicting replay deterministically without overwriting terminal evidence.
independentReview.reviewKind: sub-agent advisory; not ARCANOS backend-AI output

independentReview.finding: Python acknowledgement must mean durable protocol acceptance, not merely handler return.
recommendations: Retain run and idempotency identity across retries.
Acknowledge only accepted or proven idempotent terminal results.
Never fall back to result-through-/execute on an old or incompatible backend.
independentReview.reviewKind: sub-agent advisory; not ARCANOS backend-AI output

independentReview.finding: The initial proposal created requested runs but defined no authenticated way for Python to discover them.
recommendations: Use an executor-only atomic claim-next operation backed by durable run rows.
Do not reuse the anonymous in-memory daemon queue.
Recheck lifecycle, CLEAR, generation, executor assignment, and snapshot before returning executable work.
independentReview.reviewKind: independent sub-agent adversarial review; incorporated into Gate A revision

independentReview.finding: New endpoint authentication would remain bypassable while legacy plan reads, result reads, lifecycle mutations, and agent-capability mutations stayed anonymous.
recommendations: Authenticate or disable every equivalent legacy ActionPlan surface when Phase 2E is deployed.
Bind new plan provenance to realm and authenticated requester.
Omit destructive ActionPlan tools from MCP contexts without an authenticated principal.
independentReview.reviewKind: independent sub-agent authority review; incorporated into Gate A revision

independentReview.finding: A public digest over arbitrary command parameters cannot substantiate secret exclusion or immutable cross-language semantics.
recommendations: Persist one bounded immutable server-side snapshot per action run.
Expose only an opaque snapshot identifier for result binding.
Check snapshot and plan generation again before start and aggregation.
independentReview.reviewKind: independent sub-agent authority review; incorporated into Gate A revision

independentReview.finding: Runtime bootstrap is not a safe migration authority because web and worker both execute sequential DDL and startup may continue after failure.
recommendations: Apply Phase 2E DDL once through a checksummed out-of-band migration.
Make ordinary startup perform read-only schema verification only.
Require drain/no-overlap rollout and rollback with zero unresolved nonterminal runs.
independentReview.reviewKind: independent sub-agent persistence review; incorporated into Gate A revision
